page: Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the Microsoft Azure D4ps_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DPSv5 family, powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores running at 3.0 GHz with one thread per core, and is configured with 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable, relying on remote storage volumes for persistent disk needs.

Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities for standard compute and memory tasks, though it exhibits some limitations in specialized workloads. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests score in the average tier at 1821.99 and 7240.75 respectively, while large-block memory bandwidth read and write speeds also register in the average range. However, the instance shows weaker performance in Geekbench single-core and multi-core metrics, as well as in software compilation, text processing, and Passmark CPU tests. Notably, encryption performance is poor, scoring 651.26 on the Passmark encryption test due to the lack of advanced vector and matrix extensions, although hardware-accelerated AES cryptography is supported.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based architecture provides a resource-dense, power-efficient hardware profile suitable for scale-out workloads. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it a viable option for general-purpose applications, basic database operations, and lightweight web serving. It is best suited for workloads such as static web serving, minor database tasks, and general application hosting where extreme vector processing or high-performance single-core execution is not a primary requirement.
description: Microsoft Azure D4ps_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DPSv5 family, utilizing an ARM-based Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores running at 3.0 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and lacks local storage or GPUs, relying instead on Premium Storage capabilities. Benchmark results place its CPU and large-block memory bandwidth performance in the average tier, though it exhibits weaker results in software compilation, text processing, and encryption tasks due to the absence of advanced vector extensions. This instance offers a qualitatively cost-efficient profile for scale-out workloads, making it well-suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, and database operations that do not require intensive single-core performance or specialized instruction sets.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4ps_v5 is an ARM-based general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Ampere Altra cores, 16.0 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage capability for balanced scale-out workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4ps_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, designed for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine offering dedicated physical cores and balanced memory for efficient general-purpose and scale-out workloads.
bullet_points: Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores
16.0 GB of RAM providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Premium Storage capable with no local storage or integrated GPUs
Average performance in standard multi-core CPU and large-block memory tasks
Suitable for general-purpose applications, static web serving, and database operations
